steps.note: Phase 1: enroll under a mishear-shaped name (the Tushar trap: ASR heard 'too sharp').

steps.say: Hello there, how is it going?
The name is Toosharp. Spelled T, O, O, S, H, A, R, P.

steps.note: Branch (a) RENAME: the SAME voice protests with the real name. Route=rename (voice matches the denied record): confirm question is the relabel wording ('I had you down as Toosharp. You're Tushar, right?'); yes -> [RENAME] toosharp -> tushar, SAME id, facts follow (possessives too). This is the F4/F8 fix — pre-7-16 the lookalike guard refused this.

steps.say: No, my name is not Toosharp, my name is Tushar.
steps.expect: rename-worded confirm ('I had you down as Toosharp')
[RENAME] toosharp -> tushar id=SAME; spoken 'Fixed — Tushar it is'

steps.say: Do you remember what I build?

steps.note: Branch (c) FORK: a DIFFERENT voice (amy) gets misattributed or unknown and claims a NOVEL name via protest — matches neither tushar nor anyone -> fresh enroll fork after confirm.

steps.say: Hello Timmy, good to see you.
No, my name is not Tushar, my name is Priya.
steps.expect: standard confirm-back Priya (fork branch — NOT rename wording)
[COMMIT] priya as NEW identity; tushar untouched (no silent takeover)

steps.note: Branch (b) REBIND: priya's voice later misattributed as tushar; claiming her OWN enrolled name routes rebind -> augments priya, no new identity.

steps.say: My name is Priya, remember me?
steps.expect: if misID protest fires: confirm -> augment priya (no priya_2); else recognized outright as priya
